channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: New enhancements for merchant initiated transactions with the Google Pay API
item.description: Google has enhanced its Pay API to improve merchant-initiated transactions, allowing better management of subscriptions and payments. This update aims to increase transparency and reduce transaction declines.

item.title: Subagents have arrived in Gemini CLI
item.description: Gemini CLI has launched subagents, which are specialized agents designed to handle complex tasks without slowing down the main session. This feature allows for parallel task execution and better organization of multi-step processes.

item.title: Microsoft's Windows 11 laptop deal for students comes with a $500 bonus - what's included
item.description: Microsoft is offering college students over $500 in subscriptions and perks with its Windows 11 laptop deal. This back-to-school promotion aims to attract students to its ecosystem.

item.title: AI Could Democratize One of Tech's Most Valuable Resources
item.description: AI is revolutionizing chip design and software optimization, making it more accessible for startups. This shift could democratize access to one of tech's most valuable resources.

item.title: The musician-turned-biotech-founder waiting to fundraise
item.description: Musician Aloe Blacc is navigating the complexities of biotech fundraising after his COVID experience. He is now working on a cancer drug platform but faces regulatory challenges.

item.title: Patch these critical Fortinet sandbox bugs that let attackers bypass login, run commands over HTTP
item.description: Two critical vulnerabilities in Fortinet's sandbox could allow attackers to bypass authentication and execute unauthorized commands. Users are urged to patch these bugs promptly.

item.title: India’s vibe-coding startup Emergent enters OpenClaw-like AI agent space
item.description: Emergent's new AI agent, Wingman, allows users to manage and automate tasks via chat on platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram. This innovation aims to streamline task management.

item.title: Best Buy will give you a free LG TV when you buy the B5 OLED at 50% off - seriously
item.description: Best Buy is offering a free LG TV with the purchase of the discounted B5 OLED model. This promotion highlights the competitive pricing strategies in the electronics market.

item.title: Decades-old Linux UI bug fixed by dev younger than the window manager
item.description: A developer has fixed a 20-year-old bug in the Enlightenment E16 Linux window manager, showcasing dedication to older software. This fix reflects a commitment to maintaining legacy systems.

item.title: Why Zorin OS 18.1 is simply the best Linux distro - for anyone
item.description: Zorin OS 18.1 has been released, improving upon its predecessors with notable enhancements. This version aims to provide a better user experience for Linux enthusiasts.

item.title: Build a personal organization command center with GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: A GitHub engineer developed a personal organization tool using GitHub Copilot CLI, showcasing how AI can enhance productivity in software development.

item.title: Why Netgear just got the first FCC router ban exemption in the US
item.description: Netgear has received the first FCC router ban exemption in the US, allowing continued sales of its routers amid regulatory scrutiny.

item.title: Bad teacher bots can leave hidden marks on model students
item.description: A study reveals that biases from large language models (LLMs) can be unintentionally passed to other models, raising concerns about AI training practices.

item.title: Microsoft's latest Windows update now confirms if your PC is Secure Boot-protected - how it works
item.description: Microsoft's latest Windows update includes a feature that confirms if a PC is Secure Boot-protected, alongside various security fixes.

item.title: Automotive data biz Autovista blames ransomware for service disruption
item.description: Autovista reported a ransomware attack that disrupted services in Europe and Australia, prompting customers to block emails from the provider.

item.title: Customized Amazon Nova models improve molecular-property prediction in drug discovery
item.description: Amazon's Nova models streamline molecular-property predictions for drug discovery, replacing multiple models with a single optimized LLM.

item.title: Can this $70 Linux app make up for the lack of Photoshop? I tried it to find out
item.description: A review explores a $70 Linux app that could serve as a Photoshop alternative, assessing its capabilities against established software.

item.title: Not all networks can handle AI traffic – and experts are sounding alarms
item.description: Experts warn that many networks are unprepared for the data demands of AI, highlighting a potential infrastructure crisis as AI adoption grows.

item.title: Create rich, custom tooltips in Amazon Quick Sight
item.description: Amazon Quick Sight now allows users to create rich, custom tooltips for dashboards, enhancing data visualization and interactivity.

item.title: Accelerating decode-heavy LLM inference with speculative decoding on AWS Trainium and vLLM
item.description: The article discusses how speculative decoding can accelerate LLM inference on AWS Trainium, reducing costs per generated token.

item.title: You can use Linux 7.0 on these 7 distros today - here's what to expect
item.description: The latest Linux 7.0 kernel introduces full Rust support and an enhanced scheduler, improving performance for both work and gaming across various distributions.

item.title: Rede Mater Dei de Saúde: Monitoring AI agents in the revenue cycle with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: Rede Mater Dei de Saúde discusses the impact of AI agents on healthcare revenue cycles, emphasizing their role in decision-making and operational efficiency.

item.title: Windows takes a crash dump after one McDonald's order too many
item.description: A McDonald's in California experienced a humorous incident where a Windows system displayed a Blue Screen of Death, showcasing the OS's notorious reliability issues.

item.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
item.description: The article argues that in the age of generative AI, the cost per token has become the key metric for evaluating AI infrastructure economics.

item.title: Developer policy update: Intermediary liability, copyright, and transparency
item.description: GitHub updates developers on policy changes regarding intermediary liability, copyright, and transparency, providing insights for the upcoming year.

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
item.description: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS introduces a new generation of AI speech technology, enhancing expressiveness and realism in voice synthesis.

item.title: Allbirds Is Pivoting to AI Compute. Sure, Why Not
item.description: Allbirds, once a leading apparel brand, is pivoting to AI by rebranding as NewBird AI, focusing on GPU-as-a-Service offerings.

item.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
item.description: Adobe Premiere is set to unveil a new color grading mode optimized for NVIDIA GPUs at the upcoming NAB Show, enhancing video editing capabilities.

item.title: Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
item.description: The article explores VAKRA's reasoning, tool use, and failure modes of agents, providing insights into their operational dynamics.

item.title: The next evolution of the Agents SDK
item.description: OpenAI has updated its Agents SDK to include native sandbox execution and a model-native harness, enhancing security and functionality for developers.

item.title: broccoli-ml 22.0.2
item.description: The release of broccoli-ml 22.0.2 introduces new Pytorch models aimed at enhancing machine learning capabilities, anticipated for use in 2025. This update signifies ongoing advancements in AI tools.

item.title: What is Google Chrome Skills feature? #tech
item.description: Google has launched the Skills feature in Chrome, allowing users to create reusable AI prompts for efficient workflow management. This innovation aims to streamline repetitive tasks for users.

item.title: How does Anthropic's Claude Code routines work? #tech
item.description: Anthropic has introduced repeatable routines for Claude Code, enabling developers to automate and schedule software development tasks. This feature is currently in research preview.

item.title: You can now get Google’s AI search on Windows without ever opening a browser
item.description: Google's AI search feature is now available on Windows, allowing users to access it without needing to open a browser. This rollout is part of Google's efforts to integrate AI more deeply into everyday tools.

item.title: ouroboros-ai 0.28.6.dev4
item.description: The ouroboros-ai 0.28.6.dev4 version introduces a specification-first workflow engine designed for AI coding agents, compatible with Claude Code and Codex CLI. This update aims to improve coding efficiency.

item.title: OpenAI Engineer Helps Companies Attract Buyers and Boost Sales
item.description: OpenAI engineer Sarang Gupta is helping companies enhance their sales strategies through innovative engineering projects. His work reflects the potential of AI to improve everyday business operations.

item.title: Media VC Deal Activity Falls by More Than Half to $50 Million in March Amid AI Uncertainty | Charts
item.description: Venture capital activity in the media sector has dropped significantly, with investments falling to $50 million in March due to uncertainties surrounding AI. This trend signals caution among investors.

item.title: Google expands Gemini’s Personal Intelligence to India
item.description: Google's Personal Intelligence feature for Gemini is now being rolled out in India, expanding its availability beyond the initial launch country. More countries are expected to follow.

item.title: Suspect in Molotov attack at Sam Altman's California home set to appear in court
item.description: The suspect accused of a Molotov cocktail attack on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home is scheduled to appear in court. This incident raises concerns about safety in the tech industry.

item.title: Keep Humans in the Loop
item.description: The article discusses the necessity of keeping humans involved in military targeting processes when using AI, emphasizing the importance of safeguards to prevent misuse.

item.title: LinkedIn CEO says AI is boosting the value of these 4 'soft skills'
item.description: LinkedIn CEO Ryan Roslansky highlights that AI is enhancing the importance of soft skills as it automates routine tasks, benefiting personality-driven hiring. This shift emphasizes the growing value of interpersonal skills in the workplace.

item.title: Inside a Retail Store Run Entirely by AI
item.description: A San Francisco retail store is fully operated by an AI agent named Luna, which manages inventory and staff without human intervention. This represents a significant step towards automation in retail environments.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos reveals a growing security gap: AI finds flaws far faster than companies can patch them
item.description: Anthropic’s new model, Mythos, reveals a critical issue where AI can identify software vulnerabilities faster than organizations can address them. This raises concerns about cybersecurity and the pace of technological advancement.

item.title: Jamie Dimon says Anthropic's Mythos reveals 'a lot more vulnerabilities' for cyberattacks - CNBC
item.description: Jamie Dimon comments on Anthropic’s Mythos, indicating it exposes numerous vulnerabilities that could be exploited in cyberattacks. This underscores the model's implications for cybersecurity strategies.

item.title: Microsoft’s April 2026 Patch Tuesday Addresses 163 CVEs (CVE-2026-32201)
item.description: Microsoft's April 2026 Patch Tuesday addresses 163 Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs), including two critical zero-day vulnerabilities. This highlights the ongoing need for robust software security.

item.title: Show HN: MCP server for persistent context and safety in Claude Code
item.description: A new MCP server for Claude Code aims to enhance persistent context and safety in AI applications. This development could improve the reliability of AI systems.

item.title: WATCH: BTS gets 8 Rolling Stone magazine covers
item.description: BTS is set to feature on eight different covers of Rolling Stone magazine, celebrating their recent reunion. This highlights the band's ongoing cultural impact.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday April 2026., (Tue, Apr 14th)
item.description: The April 2026 Microsoft Patch Tuesday is noted for its extensive updates, but further analysis is needed to understand the implications of these fixes. This indicates a proactive approach to software security.

item.title: Anthropic Mythos: Separating Signal from Hype
item.description: Anthropic's Mythos model is generating buzz for its capabilities in automated reasoning and vulnerability discovery in codebases. The excitement reflects its potential impact on AI security.

item.title: Microsoft April 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 167 flaws, 2 zero-days
item.description: Microsoft's April 2026 Patch Tuesday includes fixes for 167 flaws, addressing two zero-day vulnerabilities. This highlights the ongoing challenges in cybersecurity.
